fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to indiana, pharmacy technician compact indiana, indiana pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Indiana Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Indiana Professional Licensing Agency
402 West Washington Street, Room W072
Indianapolis, IN 46204

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Indiana.

steps.title: 1. Meet Eligibility Requirements and Obtain National Certification
steps.description: Ensure you are at least 18 years old, possess a high school diploma or equivalent (GED), and have successfully passed the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) exam or the National Healthcareer Association (NHA) ExCPT exam to become a Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT).

steps.title: 2. Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: All applicants are required to complete a criminal background check for the State of Indiana prior to licensure. Follow the instructions provided by the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency on their Criminal Background Check Information page (in.gov/pla/forms-and-applications/criminal-background-check-information/) to schedule and complete this requirement.

steps.title: 3. Create Account and Apply Online via MyLicense.IN.gov
steps.description: Access the MyLicense.IN.gov portal (mylicense.in.gov), create a new user account if you don't have one. Log in and navigate to the 'Indiana Board of Pharmacy' section to start a new 'Pharmacy Technician' application.

steps.title: 4. Upload Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Electronically upload all required documents, including proof of high school diploma/GED, national certification (PTCB or NHA certificate), and any other forms requested, directly to your online application account on MyLicense.IN.gov. Do not mail documents to the Board.

steps.title: 5. Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $25.00 directly through the MyLicense.IN.gov online portal using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: 6. Await Application Review
steps.description: After submission, the Indiana Board of Pharmacy will review your application, background check results, and supporting documents. You will be notified via the portal of any deficiencies or the approval of your license.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Indiana pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Indiana have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Indiana. To work in Indiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Indiana
hero.dek: Apply online through Indiana's MyLicense.IN.gov portal (mylicense.in.gov). Create an account, select 'Board of Pharmacy,' and then 'Pharmacy Technician Application' to begin.

official_links.registration_steps: On the MyLicense.IN.gov homepage, click 'Register a New User' to create an account. Once registered, log in and navigate to the 'Indiana Board of Pharmacy' section to select and start the 'Pharmacy Technician' application.
